title: New Testament Foundations — Acts
subtitle: The Spirit-Empowered Advance of the Gospel
description: A structured study of Acts tracing the proclamation of the gospel from Jerusalem to Rome through the power of the Holy Spirit, the leadership of Peter and Paul, and the breaking of ethnic and geographic barriers.
learning_outcomes: Identify the geographic outline of Acts 1:8 and trace its fulfillment
Explain the role of the Holy Spirit in Acts' narrative
Describe the function and content of the speeches in Acts
Trace Paul's three missionary journeys and their theological significance
Explain how Acts connects to Luke as a two-volume work

theological_framework.emphasis: The Holy Spirit advancing the gospel to all nations

units.title: Jerusalem
units.description: Pentecost and the church in Jerusalem (Lessons 1–4)

units.title: To the Ends of the Earth
units.description: Paul's missions and journey to Rome (Lessons 7–12)

instructional_approach.session_structure: 7-segment lesson flow (orientation, reading, context, analysis, themes, question, close)
instructional_approach.knowledge_assessment: factual recall questions at lesson end

questions: What geographical outline does Acts 1:8 provide for the whole book?
What happened at Pentecost in Acts 2?
Who was Saul of Tarsus before his conversion?
What was the Jerusalem Council's decision about Gentile believers?
How does Acts end and where is Paul?

next_course.title: New Testament Foundations — Pauline Epistles
next_course.description: Study Paul's letters from Romans through Philemon across five letter groups
